target.name: Zipline
target.founder: Keller Rinaudo Cliffton, Keenan Wyrobek, Will Hetzler

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $600M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Nuro
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series G), post-money $6B

data.narrative:
Zipline is a Series G-stage drone and autonomous delivery company that has raised $600M+ to date. The target operates within a narrow sector containing four private peers (Nuro, Joby Aviation, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air) and no public comparables, limiting traditional peer-median benchmarking.

Given data constraints, a stage-progression method was applied as the primary valuation driver. Using total raised of $600M as a proxy for implied valuation and applying a 1.25x multiplier typical of Series G advancement, this method implies a valuation of $750M. A peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow Drones & Autonomous sector; applying an all-sector median would introduce material bias.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $638M to $862M, centered on $750M. This range reflects moderate step-up assumptions (low/high bounds apply ±15% sensitivity around the 1.25x multiplier). Key uncertainties include the accuracy of total raised as a valuation proxy, the appropriateness of a 1.25x step-up magnitude for this particular stage and sector, and the lack of validated peer benchmarks. Additional data on comparable rounds, revenue multiples, or operating metrics within the autonomous delivery sector would materially improve conviction.
